notes: Pickleheads' Dublin city index (pickleheads.com/courts/us/california/dublin) lists exactly 2 venues -- Don Biddle Community Park and Wallis Ranch Community Park -- with no single published 'N locations with M courts' summary sentence the way Palo Alto/Pleasanton have; pickleheadsStatedTotal.courts (14) is this research's own sum of Pickleheads' own per-venue figures (2 + 12), not a quote. Every other aggregator checked (DailyPB, Bounce, Global Pickleball Network) independently lists the same 2 venues and no others, and the city's own Sports Fields & Stager Community Gymnasium Reservation Policy PDF (dublin.ca.gov/DocumentCenter/View/35549) exhaustively itemizes every court/field at every other reservable Dublin facility -- Dublin Sports Grounds (baseball/soccer/softball only), Emerald Glen Park (soccer/baseball/4 tennis/2 basketball/cricket, no pickleball), Fallon Sports Park (baseball/softball/soccer/4 basketball/4 tennis/4 volleyball/cricket, no pickleball), Ted Fairfield Park (softball/soccer only) -- confirming none of them has pickleball. Flagging this explicitly because an early WebSearch AI-summary incorrectly claimed Emerald Glen, Dublin Sports Grounds, and Stager Community Gym all had pickleball; direct fetches of the official city facility pages and this PDF disproved that, so a future research pass shouldn't have to rediscover the error. No private/membership indoor pickleball club was found operating inside Dublin itself (unlike neighboring Pleasanton's Bay Club or Livermore's Tri-Valley Pickleball Club indoor sessions) -- the Livermore-based Tri-Valley Pickleball Club lists Dublin's Wallis Ranch as one of several Tri-Valley play locations on its own site but does not operate a separate Dublin facility. Dublin has zero existing entries in assets/courts-data.json or assets/venues.json (confirmed by grep) and no cities/dublin.html page yet -- it is a Phase 2 city per pickleheads-court-research-prompts.md, so both venues below are new/unmatched; matchStatus, existingId, and conflicts are omitted per this task's instructions rather than filled with 'new'/null on every record. Both venues show a real, well-explained (not unresolved) court-count discrepancy between the city's pickleball-specific page (which counts painted pickleball lines) and Pickleheads/Bounce/the city's own tennis paperwork (which count physical tennis-court slabs) -- see each venue's own notes for the full reconciliation; confirmed:true was used for both because the underlying physical facts are corroborated by multiple sources and the apparent conflict has a documented, sourced explanation rather than being genuinely unresolved.

venues.name: Don Biddle Community Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court -- pickleball lines painted on the park's 2 existing tennis courts, 2 pickleball courts striped onto each tennis-court slab (4 total)
venues.netType: Portable nets, city-owned, provided on a first-come basis; players must return and secure the net after use
venues.price: Free (drop-in/walk-up play); formal reservation fees not confirmed -- see notes
venues.hours: Daily, sunrise-10pm; lights are self-operated and auto-shutoff at 10pm. Sunrise-1pm is a citywide priority-use window: at Don Biddle this means Court 1 (one of the park's 2 tennis-court slabs) favors tennis players and Court 2 favors pickleball players. Outside sunrise-1pm, Court 1 can be reserved via dublinrecguide.com (10 days' notice required); Court 2 remains drop-in/first-come-first-served at all times.
venues.weather: Inland Tri-Valley climate -- hot, dry summers (afternoons regularly in the high 80s to 90s Fahrenheit, with occasional 100-degree spikes) and mild, wet winters. The gap in the hills toward Altamont Pass funnels strong winds through the valley -- the same corridor that powers the Altamont Pass wind farms just east of town -- so exposed courts can turn gusty by late afternoon even on calm mornings.
venues.notes: Court count reconciliation: the city's own pickleball-specific page (dublin.ca.gov/2611) and DailyPB both explicitly state 'four (4) outdoor pickleball courts, two per tennis court' -- recorded as courts:4 on that basis. Two other independent sources (Pickleheads' own venue page, and Bounce) instead say '2 outdoor concrete courts' for this same address. This is not a factual contradiction but a different counting convention: two of the city's OWN official documents (the Tennis Court Reservation Policy fee schedule, and the Sports Fields & Stager Gymnasium policy PDF) both separately list Don Biddle as having '2' tennis courts -- i.e. 2 physical asphalt slabs -- which are the same 2 slabs each striped with 2 pickleball lines to produce the 4 total playable pickleball courts the pickleball-specific page describes. Both framings are simultaneously true; a human editor should decide whether this site's sitewide 'courts' convention means physical slabs or striped pickleball lines (this record follows the more pickleball-specific primary source, matching how this repo's Palo Alto/Pleasanton records preferred the most on-topic source). Reservable: per the Tennis Court Reservation Policy PDF, of the park's 2 slabs, 'Court 1' is the reservable one (tennis-priority) and 'Court 2' is drop-in only (and is the one given pickleball priority sunrise-1pm per dublin.ca.gov/2611). Pickleheads simply states 'can't reserve the courts,' which is only true of Court 2 -- Court 1 (which also carries pickleball lines) can technically be reserved outside the sunrise-1pm window. Recorded reservable as 'Mixed' to reflect both realities. Price: every aggregator checked and the city's own 'drop-in play' framing agree walk-up pickleball is free; the Tennis Court Reservation Policy PDF confirms a $100 refundable deposit applies specifically to league/tournament/commercial-use rentals (not casual individual reservations), and repeatedly references a 'Master Fee Schedule, pages 15-16' for exact dollar amounts that could not be located as a directly fetchable document in this research -- exact per-hour individual reservation pricing, if any, is unconfirmed. Lights confirmed via the Tennis Court Reservation Policy PDF's statement that 'All lights are self-operated and are scheduled to shut off by 10:00 PM daily' for every location in its table, which includes Don Biddle Park. Address/coordinates cross-verified three ways: the official facility page (6100 Horizon Parkway), OpenStreetMap/Nominatim geocoding (37.7080993, -121.9048198), and Google Maps directly (37.7080299, -121.9049717; the listing is 'Don Biddle Community Park,' 4.8 stars/270 reviews) -- all consistent within about 15 meters of each other. googleMapsUrl's cid (3677344772142445775) was decoded from the venue's Google-internal hex place id (0x33088d963c30b4cf, read from the maps.google.com/maps/place/... link for this exact listing) and confirmed working by navigating directly to https://maps.google.com/?cid=3677344772142445775, which correctly resolves to this venue. pickleheadsRating left null -- no Pickleheads-specific star rating was found anywhere in this research; 4.8/270 is a Google rating, not a Pickleheads one, and the schema doesn't have a field for it.

venues.name: Wallis Ranch Community Park
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court -- 4 courts built with dedicated permanent pickleball lines and nets; the park's other 4 tennis courts are each additionally striped with 2 pickleball lines, adding 8 overlay pickleball courts (12 total)
venues.netType: Dedicated permanent pickleball nets on the 4 purpose-built courts; portable nets (city-owned) on the 8 tennis-court overlay courts
venues.price: Free (drop-in/walk-up play); formal reservation fees not confirmed -- see Don Biddle Community Park's notes for the citywide fee-policy detail, which applies here too
venues.hours: Daily, sunrise-10pm; lights are self-operated and auto-shutoff at 10pm. Sunrise-1pm is a citywide priority-use window: at Wallis Ranch this means 'Court 4' (one of the park's 4 shared tennis courts) favors pickleball players. Of those same 4 tennis courts, Courts 1-2 are reservable outside sunrise-1pm and Courts 3-4 remain drop-in only; the park's other 4 courts (built exclusively for pickleball, not shared with tennis) aren't covered by this tennis-court numbering in any source found.
venues.weather: Inland Tri-Valley climate -- hot, dry summers (afternoons regularly in the high 80s to 90s Fahrenheit, with occasional 100-degree spikes) and mild, wet winters. The gap in the hills toward Altamont Pass funnels strong winds through the valley -- the same corridor that powers the Altamont Pass wind farms just east of town -- so exposed courts can turn gusty by late afternoon even on calm mornings.
venues.notes: Newest park in Dublin -- opened August 16, 2025 (Pleasanton Weekly and the city's own grand-opening announcement agree), the city's 25th public park and its first built with courts purpose-made for pickleball (cost ~$11-11.8 million, funded through public facility impact fees / CIP). Court count (12) is unusually well corroborated: the city's own pickleball page (4 dedicated + 8 overlay = 12), DailyPB (matches exactly, same 4/8 split), Pickleheads (states 12 directly, though it describes all 12 as 'dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets' rather than distinguishing the 4 truly dedicated courts from the 8 tennis-overlay courts -- noted as a minor Pickleheads simplification, not treated as a blocking conflict since the total of 12 is unanimous), and teachme.to (describes '4 new lighted outdoor pickleball courts with permanent nets' plus '4 dual-use tennis courts requiring portable nets,' which reconciles exactly with the official 4+8 split once you know each dual-use tennis court carries 2 pickleball lines -- the same overlay pattern documented at Don Biddle) all agree. Reservable/hours court-numbering detail comes from the city's Tennis Court Reservation Policy PDF, which only enumerates the park's 4 SHARED tennis courts (not the 4 pickleball-only courts) -- no source found states whether the 4 dedicated pickleball-only courts have their own reservable/drop-in split or are entirely first-come-first-served; recorded reservable as 'Mixed' at the venue level pending that clarification, and this gap is exactly why courts and reservable together shouldn't be read as fully resolved down to the individual-court level. Lights confirmed both by the same city PDF's blanket 'all lights self-operated, off by 10pm' statement (Wallis Ranch Park is in the same table as Don Biddle) and independently by a teachme.to-sourced description of '4 new lighted outdoor pickleball courts.' Address (6501 Rutherford Drive) cross-verified via the city's own Facebook grand-opening post, the Tri-Valley Pickleball Club's own 'where to play' listing for this address (which separately describes '4 permanent outdoor courts and space for 4 more courts with your own nets' at this address, roughly consistent with the 4 dedicated + informal overlay picture), and Google Maps (resolves 'Wallis Ranch Community Park' to 4.4 stars/27 reviews at this address). Google Maps separately also has a lower-confidence, crowdsourced 'Wallis Ranch Pickleball Courts' pin (5.0 stars, only 1 review) a short walk south of the main park pin near the Rutherford Dr / Wallis Ranch Dr intersection -- almost certainly a sub-pin for the same park's court area rather than a distinct venue (the map shows it alongside similarly separately-pinned 'Wallis Ranch Community Park Playground' and 'Wallis Ranch Dog Park' sub-amenities, all within the same park grounds). This record deliberately uses the main, established 'Wallis Ranch Community Park' Google listing instead of that low-review sub-pin, to stay consistent with the venue name used by Pickleheads and the city; its cid (10073550993620310392) was decoded from the venue's Google-internal hex place id (0x8bcc7141b9859978) and confirmed working by navigating directly to https://maps.google.com/?cid=10073550993620310392, which correctly resolves to this venue. lat/lon (37.7288505, -121.8719013) is that same Google Maps pin; OpenStreetMap/Nominatim had no entry at all for this address as of this research, consistent with the park being well under a year old. pickleheadsRating left null -- no Pickleheads-specific star rating was found; 4.4/27 is a Google rating, not a Pickleheads one.
